name: Gumbo Z'herbes (Green Gumbo)
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Lean boneless pork, cut into bite-size pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash fresh greens and tear into small pieces. Remove large stalks and ribs.
If using frozen greens, thaw and drain before proceeding.
2. Place cleaned or thawed greens, cabbage, bay leaves, basil, thyme,
oregano, allspice, cloves, and nutmeg in a large soup pot. Add stock
and enough water to cover greens by 3 inches. Bring to a boil.
3. Reduce heat. Cover; simmer while preparing remaining ingredients.
4. Prepare Seasoning Mix: Place pork and ham pieces into the seasoning mix
(salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, paprika, onion powder, garlic powder).
Coat lightly and set aside.
5. Heat oil in a large, heavy skillet over medium-high heat. When hot,
add seasoned meat; stir until brown on all sides. Remove with a slotted spoon
and add to the greens pot. Reduce heat to medium.
6. Add onions, celery, bell pepper, and garlic to the skillet. Cook until vegetables are wilted (about 5 minutes).
7. Remove vegetables with a slotted spoon and add them to the greens pot.
Stir in sugar; simmer uncovered for one hour.
8. Season to taste with salt, black pepper, and cayenne. Cover and simmer for an additional 2 hours.
9. About 10 minutes before serving, gently stir in oysters with their liquor,
green onions, and parsley. Cook until edges of oysters curl.
10. Spoon about 1/2 cup rice into each soup bowl. Ladle the gumbo over the rice.
Top with a pinch of file powder. Serve 8-10 servings.
